translated: The hunter said that if he had known that the animal would die and they would bring it and roast it like that, he would not have killed it. He said to them, ‘I would have told you to roast it like this so that we could all eat it together.’

ref: At that moment Jesus said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people.
text: Saŋa maŋa nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, “Mɛ‐Sɛ, fʋ gyɛ sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, n yɩn fʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ taa falɛ asɩn baala anyaasɩnpʋ yɛ anyipu sʋ, nɩɩ fʋ lɛɛ ɩmʋ kaapʋ nbii wuribi.
translated: When Jesus saw the crowd, he said, “My son, you are truly the Son of God, and I have come to you, so that you may go and preach to the people of this world, and you will see them turn to God.”

ref: Go only to the people of Israel, because they are like a flock of lost sheep.
text: Amaa fan kpee Ɩsɩrayɩ abi mʋ nɩɩ ba du fɛɛ nbʋlʋpʋ fɔyɩsa mʋ asɛ.
translated: But the people of Israel did not listen to him because of their broken spirit and hard labor.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
text: Nɩɩ ɩkʋ mɔɔ ɩ tɩyɛ iwii tɔ, nɩɩ iwii mʋ ɩ dan nyan kɩtaa ɩmʋ.
translated: If the sun shines on you, you should also shine on others.

ref: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
text: Nɩɩ asa ba sɩlɛ lɩɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa sʋ kpini bɛɛ ba Yesu asɛ, nɩɩ ba taa alɔpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba dɛ ɩkalan sʋ bɩya mʋ.
translated: If someone has a brother who sins against him, he should go and tell him his fault privately.

ref: Suddenly a man with leprosy came and knelt in front of Jesus. He said, “Lord, you have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɔ kibootoo ba muŋa mu amulii sʋ mu ansi tɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ nɩɩ ɩ pɩrɛ fʋ mʋ, fɩɩ taalɛ yɛgɛ mɛ n‐yɩɩ ɩ lɔŋɔ sʋ.”
translated: The man said that if his mother had not given birth to him and raised him until he grew up, he would have said to her, “Mother, thank you for bringing me into this world.”

ref: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
text: Nɩɩ ɔ kɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ kyaabɔɔ mʋ gyaŋɛ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, “Fan kɩɩ! Banɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Mi‐nyi yɛ mi‐supu ana.
translated: When he reached the place where he was to build his house, he said, “Stop here! From now on this place is mine and my children’s.”

ref: Everyone answered, “We and our own families will take the blame for his death!”
text: Asa mʋ kpini ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Yɛgɛ mu lewu kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ ɩ baa ɩ gyan anɛ yɛ anɛ‐bii ana sʋ!”
translated: The hawk came to the hen and said, “If I catch you eating my grain, I will kill you and your children!”

ref: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
text: “Fanɛ anɩmʋ nɩɩ mu‐bii ɩɩ kʋlɛ mʋ bodobodo kɛ ɔ taa kubuu sa mʋ?
translated: If a child is born and grows up, will he still be called a child?

ref: Finally, Jesus returned to his disciples and said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
text: Nɩɩ o bwii ba mʋ abɩɩlapʋ asɛ ba taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan san fan dɛ fɛn fʋʋtɛ nɩn? Fan kɩɩ, saŋa mʋ fʋʋ fɛɛ ba taa mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ waa alibi awaapʋ abaa tɔ.
translated: When he got up and went to his in-laws and they asked him, “What did you come to do here?” He said, “I have come so that you give me your daughter, Daadimaadi Obii-nyɩnsa, to be my wife.”

ref: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
text: “Anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ kesintin gyipu yɛ kanyaasɩn wuya? Mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ taa mʋ waa mʋ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ fɛɛ ɔ sa bamʋ agyitɔ saŋa kyɛɛkyɛɛ tɔ.
translated: Why is it that a child’s stubbornness is greater than an elder’s wisdom? It is because when a child refuses to listen to advice and insists on doing as he pleases, his heart becomes hardened so that even if you cut open his chest and sprinkle white chalk into it, it will not change.

ref: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
text: “Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fii wu keyuubi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fʋ bɩrɩsa kinsi tɔ, nɩɩ fu mee wu kagyakpoti mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dɛ fu kinsi tɔ?
translated: If you say that your friend’s yam is not good, is it because your own yam is better?

ref: They were able to see, and Jesus strictly warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: Nɩɩ bamʋ ansi i bugi. Nɩɩ Yesu da bamʋ amu tɔ kenken yɛ, “Fan man tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ sa ɔkʋ.”
translated: They were sitting by the fire. When Jesus saw them he said to them, “Come and I will make you fishers of men.”

ref: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
text: “Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n biti n tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ mɛ bɩna sʋ mʋ yɛ, ‘Fan taa lɩɩ mɛ sʋ, fanɛ nɩɩ mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ nnɔ waa mʋ. Fan natɛ loo awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen mʋ nɩɩ ii mee dun, nɩɩ mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ dɛɛla yɩla Sɩtaanɛ maa mʋ nbɔɔ mʋ tɔ.
translated: One day the spider went to his father-in-law’s house to greet him, but when he arrived he didn’t find him, so he said, ‘Let me just sit here, let me wait for him—Mr. Ibwaare will certainly come.’ While he sat there he began to feel very hungry, waiting for Mr. Ibwaare, but instead Satan came and gave him some food to eat.

ref: They saw him and worshiped him, but some of them doubted.
text: Ba maa wu Yesu mʋ, ba muŋa sun mʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ akʋ ba gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ yɛ man gyɛ mʋ nɩn.
translated: They saw Jesus and recognized him, so they ran to tell others that they had seen him.

ref: when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk.
text: Nɩɩ asa ana akʋ ba sʋla mʋgʋlɛ‐mʋgʋlɛ‐nyɩn boobi kʋ bɛɛ bɩya Yesu.
translated: When he saw the great crowd that had come to him, he said to Philip, “Where shall we buy bread for these people to eat?”

ref: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?” They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
text: Gyɔn kekyugyee mʋ, fɩnɛ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ? Ɩ kyɩna lɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩn abɛɛ ɩ lɩɩ asa asɛ nɩn?” Ba tɔwɛ bamʋ wʋlɛ‐wʋlɛ tɔ yɛ, “Nɩɩ an kan tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Kɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩn’, ee biti ɔ taasɛ anɛ yɛ, ‘Nɩɩ mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fan man kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii?’
translated: The tortoise said, “What is this thing? Is it not the thing that looks like this thing here?” Then the others replied, “When someone says, ‘What is this thing?’ you don’t immediately answer, ‘Why do you bring this thing to spoil my name?’”

ref: Jesus replied, “How then could the Spirit lead David to call the Messiah his Lord? David said,
text: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Ɩ waa nɛnɛ sʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu tɔ Dawidi tɩɩ mʋ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ?’ Lɩɩ fɛɛ Dawidi tɔwɛ yɛ,
translated: Then Jesus asked them, “How is it that the Messiah is David’s son? For David himself says,”

ref: The man talked about it so much and told so many people, that Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He had to stay away from the towns, but people still came to him from everywhere.
text: Amaa ɔnyɩn mʋ dan lɩɩ kpee katɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ ɛɛ da kibonbon ɛɛ sa asa yɛ Yesu ɩ kyɛ mʋ kibootoo. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Yesu man bɩla ɛɛ nyɛ ee loo kadɛ kʋ tɔ tentengilen. O kpee katɩn mʋ nɩɩ i du diin, yɛgɛ asa bɛɛ lɩɩ katɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ bɛɛ ba mʋ asɛ.
translated: But he did not want to build his house like others, because he had heard that Jesus would come to his village. So he said that Jesus would not find him living in a house like others. He wanted to build his house in the bush, saying that others would build their houses like others and come to his place.
